/about.name: About Axsuma
/about.copy:
Axsuma is a UK-based provider of corporate administration and governance services. We support businesses, family offices, funds, and their advisors with their administration needs at every stage of the company lifecycle, from entity formation through company secretarial, M&A deal support, accounting and tax compliance, and payroll in the United Kingdom.

What we do 
We help our clients meet statutory and regulatory obligations with confidence, while enhancing operational efficiency. By implementing robust governance frameworks, we enable sustainable growth and long-term value creation. Our services are designed to provide clarity and control throughout every stage of a company’s lifecycle.

How we are different 
Accountability drives everything we do. We combine institutional-grade technical expertise with pragmatic, hands-on delivery. Each assignment has a named owner, decisions are shaped by commercial realities, and work is executed thoroughly and on schedule. We prioritise clarity, control and consistent follow-through, the factors that make a tangible difference to our clients.

We do not provide legal advice or structuring. Instead, we work closely with clients’ lawyers and accountants to ensure decisions are implemented cleanly, accurately and without friction. Our role is to take ownership of execution. Translating professional advice into precise, dependable delivery.

Why Choose Axsuma?
We combine human judgement with structured processes to deliver clarity, accountability, and dependable outcomes. Our sector expertise allows us to anticipate challenges, remove friction, and provide peace of mind across every mandate.

/about/our-approach.name: Our Approach
/about/our-approach.copy:
Our clients and business partners come to us with challenges that carry real consequences. Getting it wrong is not an option. We exist for the moments that matter when decisions have impact, timelines are tight, and ambiguity is unacceptable.
While the industry talks about process, platforms, and coverage, we focus on what truly matters to clients: judgement, accountability, and delivery. Corporate services have drifted into fragmentation. Work is distributed, ownership is unclear, and clients are left exposed. Our approach is deliberately designed to close that gap.

We Take Responsibility
Not shared. Not diluted. Not buried in a workflow. We take clear, human ownership of every mandate from the first instruction to the final outcome. Someone is always accountable and nothing disappears into a system. When complexity arises, we step in, take control, and keep work moving.

Transparency in Pricing
Our approach to fees is grounded in transparency, accountability, and commercial alignment. All costs are agreed upfront with fixed fees applied to clearly defined workstreams wherever possible. This provides clients with full cost certainty, mitigates the risk of unexpected overruns, and enables effective management of overall spend.

We Finish What We Start
Delivery is not a phase. It is the outcome. We stay until the job is done properly, on time, without friction, and without dropped balls. No fuss. No theatre. Just work done well.

Not the biggest.
Not the loudest.
The most dependable.

/about/meet-the-team.name: Meet the team
/about/meet-the-team.copy:
Our founders and senior team bring decades of experience from Magic Circle law firms, Big 4 accountancy practices and leading corporate service providers. They combine institutional expertise with a relationship-first approach, so you get technically robust, regulator-ready advice delivered with commercial sense and personal accountability.


Gurpreet is a strategic HR leader and trusted advisor who works closely with founders, executives, and leadership teams across high-growth and international businesses. With experience spanning enterprise technology, SaaS, and private equity-backed environments, she focuses on building practical, scalable people strategies that drive performance, engagement, and long-term growth.

She brings a strong balance of strategic thinking and hands-on delivery, with a commercial and pragmatic approach. Gurpreet has led global People functions, built HR operating models from the ground up, and supported organisations through periods of rapid growth and change, including M&A, organisational design, and international expansion.

/about/sectors-we-serve.name: Sectors We Serve
/about/sectors-we-serve.copy:
We provide operational clarity and robust governance frameworks across a wide range of sectors. Our clients trust us to manage complexity, ensure compliance, and deliver outcomes with precision and accountability.

Businesses and Corporates
We support companies of all sizes, from start-ups to multinational corporations, ensuring statutory and regulatory obligations are met efficiently. Our approach enables management teams to focus on growth and strategy while we handle the operational details.

Family Offices
We provide family offices with bespoke governance, entity management, and reporting solutions. By simplifying administrative complexity, we help families protect wealth, structure investments effectively, and maintain control over their affairs.

Funds and Investment Vehicles
Our team helps investment funds and special purpose vehicles navigate corporate administration and compliance with confidence. From entity formation to ongoing reporting, we provide transparency, accountability, and operational reliability at every stage.

Advisors and Professional Partners
We work closely with lawyers, accountants, and other advisors to deliver a seamless service to their clients. By taking responsibility for operational execution, we allow advisors to focus on strategic advice while ensuring operational excellence is maintained.

/services/company-secretarial.name: Company Secretarial
/services/company-secretarial.copy:
The role of company secretaries, in-house legal teams, and compliance teams is becoming increasingly complex. We support our clients as an extension of their team, whenever and however they need us. This could be interim support during busy periods, specialist project assistance, a fully outsourced company secretarial function, all tailored support to suit your specific needs.

Our company secretarial services ensure your company remains compliant with the Companies Act 2006, Companies House filing obligations, and corporate governance best practices. Our team manages the complete framework of statutory obligations, including maintaining registers, filing annual confirmations, managing director changes, handling persons with significant control (PSC) notifications, and keeping corporate records current and compliant.

We can be formally appointed as your corporate Company Secretary or provide flexible company secretarial support. Whether you are a small private company or a larger corporate structure, our services adapt to meet your needs.

Our Core Company Secretarial Services Include:
Maintaining and updating statutory registers for shareholders, directors, and PSCs 
Preparing and filing Confirmation Statements 
Providing a prestigious London registered office and, optionally, acting as your appointed corporate secretary 
Managing governance changes such as officer appointments, share transfers, and restructures 
Drafting legal documents, including resolutions, minutes, powers of attorney, and filings 

Registered Office and Mail Forwarding Services
Every company must have a registered office for statutory notices and legal correspondence. Axsuma provides a prestigious London address that appears on all Companies House filings, public documents, and statutory notices. We receive all statutory correspondence, including documents from Companies House (such as confirmation statement reminders and annual filing notices) and HMRC communications (tax assessments, penalties, enquiries).

Our mail forwarding service includes email notifications with scanned copies of all mail received, ensuring you stay fully informed without the need for a physical office presence.

Transfer-In and Migration from Other Providers
If your company is currently managed by another provider, Axsuma can seamlessly take over company secretarial support. We coordinate the transition, retrieve all statutory information, verify register accuracy, identify pending filings, and assume responsibility for ongoing governance and compliance.
During the transfer-in process, we conduct a comprehensive compliance review:
Ensuring confirmation statements are up to date 
Verifying accounts are properly filed 
Checking director and PSC details for accuracy 
Confirming charges are correctly registered 

Any gaps or regulatory exposure are addressed with a remediation plan to bring your company fully into compliance.

/services/ma.name: M&A Overview
/services/ma.copy:
Readiness Solutions for Complex Corporate Transactions 

UK Ready-Made Packages for M&A, Restructuring, and Joint Ventures

In the fast-paced environment of complex corporate transactions, timing is critical. Complex deal structures, regulatory hurdles, and aggressive timelines mean there is zero margin for administrative delay. We know that as dealmakers, your focus belongs on negotiation, strategy, and execution — not on the distraction of the corporate administration aspects.

Axsuma streamlines the process. We support lawyers, corporates, and private equity firms with pre-established corporate structures purpose-built for fast-moving M&A, restructuring, and carve-outs. We handle the essential administrative groundwork, providing the speed and structural certainty required to close the deal. Beyond completion, we ensure all entities remain fully compliant across their regulatory, accounting, tax, and payroll obligations.

Transaction-Ready Corporate Stacks

Accelerate deal execution with pre-established structures.

Delays in entity formation, governance setup, or appointing directors can create unnecessary friction during sensitive deal phases. Our solution eliminates these risks.

We offer ready-made HoldCo, MidCo, and BidCo entities available for immediate reservation. These structures are securely held to order exclusively for law firms and advisors, allowing you to focus on deal structuring and negotiation without administrative bottlenecks.

The Ready-Made Corporate Stack
Our fully aligned three-tier acquisition structure is configurable to specific transaction requirements. All entities are established with clean corporate records, dormant status, and are maintained in good standing.

HoldCo: ultimate parent entity; structured to support ownership, financing, and long-term governance.

MidCo: intermediary holding entity; designed for structural flexibility and risk segregation.

BidCo: acquisition vehicle; purpose-built to execute the purchase and receive assets or shares at closing.



How It Works: Reservation & Hold-to-Order 

Immediate Reservation - Entities can be reserved instantly upon instruction, securing exclusivity for your transaction.

Held to Order for Up to 6 Months (extendable by agreement) - We maintain the corporate stack on your behalf - dormant, compliant, and ready - while deal negotiations progress.

Activation on Demand - Upon confirmation, entities are fully activated and transferred in accordance with your instructions and transaction timeline.


Designed for Lawyers & Transaction Advisors

We operate as an infrastructure partner, not a legal decision-maker – supporting your work with speed and precision. Our offering is purpose-built for:

Lawyers managing complex and time-sensitive M&A transactions
Private equity and investment advisors
Corporate development and in-house legal teams
Cross-border and multi-jurisdictional deal structures


Key Benefits

Reduced time to signing and closing
Predictable transaction readiness
Clean, pre-established corporate records
Flexible holding period aligned with deal timelines
Confidential and controlled reservation process

Single SPVs

If you are seeking a sole SPV for an upcoming transaction, we also retain standalone entities for immediate reservation on the same held to order basis.

Transaction-Ready. When You Need It.

Whether preparing for a competitive bid, conditional signing, or regulatory approval phase, our ready-made HoldCo–MidCo–BidCo stacks give you the structural certainty to move decisively.

It will be necessary to conduct some compliance checks in accordance with the UK Money Laundering Regulations upon transfer of ownership at completion.

/services/accounting-tax.name: Accounting & Tax
/services/accounting-tax.copy:
Finance and tax functions are often fragmented across multiple providers, creating inefficiency, inconsistent reporting, and unnecessary administrative burden. We deliver a unified, scalable solution designed to simplify compliance, enhance visibility, and support better financial decision-making across jurisdictions and structures.
Our services include:
Statutory accounts preparation and financial reporting in line with relevant accounting standards 
Corporate tax compliance, filings, and advisory support, including optimisation of reliefs and allowances 
Monthly and quarterly management accounts with tailored, multi-entity and multi-currency reporting 
Outsourced finance functions, including bookkeeping, payroll, and VAT compliance

/services/payroll-hr.name: Payroll & HR
/services/payroll-hr.copy:
Payroll and HR management is increasingly complex, driven by evolving legislation, real-time reporting requirements, and heightened expectations around accuracy and employee experience. We provide a streamlined, solution that reduces administrative burden while ensuring full compliance with HMRC and employment regulations.

Our approach combines precise payroll delivery with practical HR support, giving you a single, coordinated service that integrates seamlessly with your wider operations. Whether you are managing a growing UK workforce or supporting inbound entities, we deliver consistency, control, and clear visibility.

What we support with:
Payroll processing and administration
End-to-end payroll delivery, including calculations, payslips, and payment coordination 
HMRC compliance and RTI reporting
Management of PAYE, National Insurance, and all Real Time Information (RTI) submissions 
Auto-enrolment and pensions
Administration of workplace pension schemes, including enrolment, contributions and compliance 
HR administration support
Employee onboarding and offboarding, contracts of employment, and maintenance of personnel records 
Statutory payments and leave
Management of statutory sick pay (SSP), maternity/paternity pay and associated entitlements 
Advisory and best practice
Guidance on UK employment processes, policies and operational efficiency 

The result is a robust, compliant payroll and HR function that supports your people while enabling your business to operate with confidence.

/services/compliance-governance.name: Compliance & Governance
/services/compliance-governance.copy:
We support organisations in establishing and operating robust, proportionate governance frameworks that stand up to regulatory scrutiny and scale with growth. Our focus is practical delivery—ensuring governance is not only compliant, but embedded, efficient, and aligned to the realities of how your business operates.
We work with companies, funds, family offices and their advisers across a range of jurisdictions, often in complex or fast-moving environments. Whether you require targeted advisory input, interim support, or a fully outsourced company secretarial function, our model is designed to integrate seamlessly and deliver with precision.
What we support with:
Governance framework design and optimisation
Review, design and implementation of governance structures, policies and supporting documentation 
Company secretarial services
End-to-end support including statutory filings, annual compliance and provision of named Company Secretary where required 
Board and meeting support
Coordination and delivery of board, committee and shareholder meetings, including agendas, papers and minutes 
Interim and outsourced Company Secretary support
Flexible resourcing to bridge gaps, manage peak periods or fully outsource the function 

The outcome is a governance framework that delivers clarity, control and confidence—without adding unnecessary complexity.

/services/compliance-governance/id-verif.name: ID Verification
/services/compliance-governance/id-verif.copy:
Companies House Identity Verification
From 18 November 2026, identity verification under the UK’s Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act (ECCTA) became a legal requirement, initiating a 12-month transition period for compliance.
Axsuma is a Companies House Authorised Corporate Service Provider (ACSP), authorised to carry out identity verification on behalf of clients.
As part of the UK Government’s drive to enhance transparency and strengthen trust in corporate structures, the ECCTA requires all directors, LLP members, Persons with Significant Control (PSCs), and individuals filing on behalf of companies to verify their identity with Companies House. Upon successful verification, each individual will be issued with a unique personal code.
Where a corporate director or a corporate entity is listed as a PSC (i.e. a Relevant Legal Entity), details of at least one director of that entity must also be provided for identity verification.
The unique reference code is assigned to the individual rather than their role or any specific company. As such, individuals holding multiple roles (for example, acting as both a director and PSC, or serving across multiple entities) are only required to complete the verification process once.
By choosing to verify through an ACSP, your ID documents and verification evidence are processed and stored by the ACSP rather than within the GOV.UK One Login identity system. This ensures your documentation is managed within a single regulated professional services relationship, rather than a centralised government identity platform.
We offer a streamlined, fully supported process to help you meet these requirements efficiently and with confidence. You can begin the process online, with expert guidance available at every stage.
We will then proceed to the registration stage. Once this is complete, you will receive a unique code from Companies House.
Please complete and submit the below:
Individuals to Verify – Amendments
Amend wording under “Individuals to Verify” to read “Enter the details for each person. You can add details for additional individuals to be verified below”
Your Details section - Amendments
Replace “Who is ordering this verification?” with “Please provide details of who the receipted invoice should be issued to below”

Add – Full address field

/services/compliance-governance/uk-afs.name: UK Address for Service
/services/compliance-governance/uk-afs.copy:
Address for Service for Intellectual Property Rights

We provide a UK Address for Service (UK AFS) for intellectual property matters, supporting compliance with the requirements of the UK Intellectual Property Office and use in UK IP proceedings, ensuring the effective receipt of formal notices and correspondence.

What it is
An address for service is the UK address to which the IPO or third parties may send correspondence relating to an application or registered right, and where documents in IP proceedings may be delivered.

What we do
Receive and log IP-related notices and correspondence
Scan and securely forward documents to your nominated contact
Provide documented confirmation of receipt where required
Coordinate next steps with your legal advisers on request

Get started
Share your requirements via the contact form and we will connect you with the appropriate specialist.

/international.name: International Expansion
/international.copy:
For over 20 years, our specialists have supported companies establishing and operating in the UK. We know that many businesses are not entering a single market but expanding across multiple jurisdictions at the same time and require a coordinated multi jurisdictional approach to expansion.

We support clients entering new markets by combining UK led coordination with a trusted international network of local service providers. This enables us to navigate administrative, legal, regulatory and cultural complexities, and removing friction from the expansion process.

Our network
Our partners are firms we have worked with over many years, selected for their proven capability, responsiveness and alignment with our standards. This ensures consistent delivery while retaining the benefit of local expertise.

Scope of support
Entity Formation
Company Secretarial & Registered Office 
Accounting & Tax 
Global payroll
Employer of record (EOR)
International HR

Commercial approach
We manage engagements centrally to ensure efficient execution, competitive pricing and seamless coordination across jurisdictions, so your expansion remains compliant and commercially effective, allowing you to focus on growth

Get started
Share your plans with us and we will design a coordinated, jurisdiction-specific approach aligned to your objectives.

Whether you are onboarding a new structure, managing ongoing entity administration, or requesting ad hoc support, everything is housed in one place with a clear, structured workflow. /cookie-policy.name: Cookie Policy
/cookie-policy.copy:
Axsuma Cookie Policy

What Are Cookies?
Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help us improve your experience by remembering your preferences, analysing website usage, and ensuring core functionality.

How We Use Cookies
We use the following types of cookies:
Strictly Necessary: Essential for the website to function. 
Performance and Analytics: Help us understand how visitors use our site and improve its performance. 
Functional: Remember your choices to provide a personalised experience. 
Advertising/Targeting: We do not use third-party advertising cookies. 

Third-Party Cookies
Some cookies may be set by third-party service providers, such as analytics platforms, in accordance with their privacy policies.

Your Choices
You can manage or disable cookies in your browser settings at any time. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect website functionality.

More Information
For further information about how we use cookies, please contact us at enquiries@axsuma.co.uk

Effective Date: [Insert date]
